summary: The test suite provides solid unit test coverage of core semantic versioning logic (29 unit tests for parseVersion, formatVersion, parseCommit, determineBumpType, bumpVersion, generateChangelog, etc.) and validates GitHub Actions workflow structure. However, it has critical gaps: (1) No tests for the actual CLI entry point bump-version.ts — the main() function, getVersionFile(), getCommitLog(), file I/O operations, and environment variable handling are completely untested; (2) No integration tests for end-to-end version bumping (reading VERSION/package.json, parsing commits, writing updated files, generating CHANGELOG.md); (3) No tests for error scenarios in real usage (missing version files, invalid JSON, git failures, write permission issues); (4) Test fixtures referenced in run-tests.ts (commits-feat.txt, etc.) are not shown or verified; (5) workflow-structure.test.ts validates YAML structure but not actual workflow execution or step outputs. While unit tests are well-organized with clear describe/test blocks and proper TypeScript types, the suite would miss regressions in the actual script behavior when called from GitHub Actions or the CLI.
